---
title: "Salary & Compensation trends"
format: html
jupyter: python3
execute:
  echo: true
  warning: false
  message: false
---

## Research Questions
This section focuses on the following questions:

1. How do salaries differ across AI vs. non-AI careers?
2. What regions offer the highest-paying jobs in AI-related and traditional careers?
3. Are remote jobs better paying than in-office roles?
4. What industries saw the biggest wage growth in 2024?

## Load Data

```{python}
import pandas as pd
import numpy as np
import plotly.express as px

df = pd.read_csv("data/processed/lightcast_cleaned.csv")
df.head()
```

## 1. How do salaries differ across AI vs. non-AI careers
```{python}
ai_keywords = [
    "ai", "artificial intelligence", "machine learning", "ml",
    "deep learning", "nlp", "computer vision", "generative ai",
    "data scientist", "machine learning engineer", "ai engineer"
]

df["TITLE_CLEAN"] = df["TITLE_CLEAN"].astype(str)

df["ai_flag"] = df["TITLE_CLEAN"].str.lower().apply(
    lambda x: "AI-related" if any(k in x for k in ai_keywords) else "Non-AI"
)

salary_ai = (
    df.groupby("ai_flag")["salary_annual"]
      .mean()
      .reset_index()
      .sort_values("salary_annual", ascending=False)
)

fig = px.bar(
    salary_ai,
    x="ai_flag",
    y="salary_annual",
    title="Average Annual Salary: AI-related vs. Non-AI Careers",
    labels={"ai_flag": "Career Type", "salary_annual": "Average Annual Salary"}
)
fig.show()
```
The bar chart shows that AI-related careers have a slightly higher average annual salary than non-AI careers. 
In this sample, the average salary for AI-related roles is around 120k, while non-AI roles are slightly lower at about 116k. 
This suggests that jobs involving AI and machine learning skills tend to be compensated at a somewhat higher level.

However, the salary gap is not very large, which may indicate that many non-AI positions in the dataset are also high-paying technical or business roles. 
Overall, the figure supports the idea that AI-related jobs offer a salary premium, but the difference appears to be moderate rather than dramatic.


```{python}

```

```{python}

```

```{python}

```

```{python}

```


```{python}

```
